mission_02:
## What You Will Learn

Before training any model, you must split your data properly. A poor split can create unrealistic performance estimates and lead to overconfidence in your model.

### Key Concepts
- **Train/Test Split**: Divides data into training set (for learning) and test set (for unbiased evaluation).
- **Stratification**: Ensures both splits maintain the same class proportions as the original data. Critical for imbalanced datasets.
- **random_state**: A seed for reproducibility. Same seed = same split every time. Essential for debugging and collaboration.
- **Data Leakage Prevention**: Never look at test data during training or preprocessing decisions.

### Why This Matters
Without stratification, you might randomly get a test set with zero examples of a rare class. Without a random seed, your results become impossible to reproduce.
